slots: Nagpur makes the ideal base — two hours through rural Vidarbha brings you to Navegaon Bandh lake and the park headquarters.
The reservoir boat trip circles forested islands — scan for storks, cormorants, and occasional mugger crocodiles basking on banks.
Climb the park watchtower for golden-hour views over teak canopy — langurs often chatter in the treetops below.
Guided foot trails explore mixed forest — morning is best for deer, wild boar, and bird activity. Tiger sightings are possible but require luck.
Vidarbha afternoons are warm — rest at the rest house or visitor centre exhibits before an evening exploration.
Short village strolls reveal paddy fields and traditional homes — try simple Vidarbha thali with bhakri and spicy thecha.
Circle the lake shore for last photographs — winter brings additional waterfowl; summer concentrates mammals near water.
Head back to Nagpur for evening flights or an optional Tadoba extension eastward.
Combine Navegaon with Pench or Tadoba for a longer Vidarbha wildlife circuit.
